channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program — 82 replies — 97.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
Theyre ending the Chainlink BUILD program

Open thread • Replies: 82 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:02 UTC

OP

LMAO!!!!!

https://chain.link/blog/build-program-evolution

Early replies (first 3)

62417511
xrp is the chosen coin…it’s undeniable at this point

62417512
Come on man, this shit still spamming after 8 years? Just give it up. There are thousands of other coins.

62417514
"Chainlink Labs is transitioning from payments made in project tokens to focusing on commercial agreements involving fees paid in LINK or other liquid assets that can be readily converted to LINK."

Build members will buy my bags?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62419959
>>62417503
Nomore free rides for build startups, now they need to pay?

Not sure if bullish or bearish desu

62420074
>>62419194
I'm a fuddie but you are right. If you're a holder then acoomulate more and lower your price targets next cycle.

62420086
>>62417888
Checked, counting the chickens before they hatch

62420241
>>62419888
Checked, and correct. We are fucked, well it was nice thinking i would get rich one day

62420825
>>62419888
>>62420241

You faggots are like the riffraff who like to go to the casino to play your game of choice but the motivation to get you out the door is some stupid fucking promotional thing. Like you like to play poker or blackjack, and you are even good at these games, but you only decide to go cuz there’s some free slot play for $10 for signing up for a their casino card.

It’s like you faggots think that these build rewards would make up half your profit from investing in link. Link goes to $500 and you have 5 million dollars, and your expecting some bullshit build token to make you 2.5 million dollars also?

62420893
>>62420825
The fuck is that analogy lmao. This isn't a casino you dumb faggot, real people invested real money into an asset that promised certain things by certain dates and they have not only underdelivered but are starting to actively ignore those promises.
>Build rewards were always a nothingcarrot anyways!
you are such a raped goy its not even funny.

62420922
>>62419676
Build was literally start ups getting free link services (funded by the team selling link) in exchange for token supplies that may be worth something one day. It's expressly for those who couldn't afford the services, so no they won't just use link instead.

62421000
>>62420825
Ive been holding link since 2017, 2 man team of stuttering pot heads. Lower your tone when talking to me biy

62421047
>>62420893
I didn’t invest in Link in early 2018 cuz of stupid fucking Build rewards. Were they even a thing in 2018? Or were they after the fact like 2019-20?

If I staked and had build rewards I would have given them to hookers cuz they are irrelevant to the link investment we made. In no world would any Build token or combined build tokens would have made up more than 10% of the profits we would get from holding link.

62421081
>>62420825
>Link goes to $500

best we can do is $1.50 in 2030

62421085
>>62417503
It's funny that the BUILD program is the only way I've ever actually had to interact with the ecosystem that I invested so much into.
I had to learn how to use a DEX to unload these shitcoin bags lmao.
It was a nice little bonus though. All up I ended up getting about $12,400 from it.

62421094
>>62418828
Based

62421109
>>62420922
"... or be converted to Link".

Another illiterate brown imbecile.

62421512
>>62421109
Nice so our $194.50 each combined total of rugged build shitcoin airdrops can go to purchasing an extra 20 link tokens for the reserve instead.. Marvelous!.................. KABOOOOOOOOM BULLISH CHAINLINK! $LINK #LINKTHEWORLD

62421528
>>62420922
What they're saying is that those token supplies have performed terribly and aren't even worth holding. Every single one of them. It's not a good look for the ecosystem, but the good thing is it won't affect the price because nothing does.

62421600
>>62421528
Not really. They're saying that projects aren't launching native tokens any more, and so aren't eligible for the current BUILD program. So they're finding different ways to encourage growth (particularly in the very small number of projects that actually succeed) while still making sure they PAY UP.

62421684
Chain Link Labs, "Our build coins aren't worth shit, so we're canceling build forever, thanks"!

62421956
>>62421600
The converted proceeds still should go directly towards the ecosystem participants (ie. NOPs and stakers), not to Chainlink Labs' own personal Reserve.
CLL should be adopting a more behind-the-curtains presence but instead they route the entire network through themselves. Not really neutral.

62422159
>>62420825
>It’s like you faggots think that these build rewards would make up half your profit from investing in link.

Thats how the turboshills hyped it up here.

Reserve, a flop.
Ccip revenue a flop
PAL a flop
Build rewards a flop
AMA a flop
Link staking 2.0 a flop
Eric schmidt, Tom szach, all flops

0 will flow to token holders , its all carrots. Only ones left are donkeys and the one on the cart.

62422217
>>62421109
Yes it won't happen. The projects can't afford link and their tokens have no value, so that means there just won't be any value coming into the ecosystem from it. Never was tbqh.

62422274
>>62421684
>>62421956
>>62422159
>>62422217

xerpies crying overtime again

62422290
>>62417503
Never mind, they were all dogshit anyway.
Require all transactions to be made in dollars and use that to fill the reserves and drive up the value of the token.

62422638
>>62421600
Ah that's better

>>62421956
And this is true. The reserve does nothing for us whereas at least the build rewards were supposed to go to us.

Does this announcement mean there is no build season 2 or subsequent though? That's what I took from. The announcement ie we get nothing now.

62422640
>>62422290
The reserve doesn't help holders, it only helps Chainlink the company

62422668
>>62422638
>The announcement ie we get nothing now.
another illterate brown xerpie

62422671
I am so glad I have never touched this piece of shit. The level of delusion here is unprecedented

62422754
>>62422671
You own XRP and you’re gay

62422761
>>62422671
baggie delusion doesn't bother me personally, it's the fact that shills were vehemently shilling and defending BUILD for years that infuriates me, and now they act like it was never a big deal
it's always 180 degree pivot and then denial and deflection, it's sickening

62422772
>>62422761
You've been reading shills defend Build for years? Why would you do that?

62423044
>>62422274
>never debate the point
>always ad hominem / adem / xerpie

item.title: IT'S THE FUCKING TOP. SELL. SELL IT ALL. — 18 replies — 72.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
IT'S THE FUCKING TOP. SELL. SELL IT ALL.

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:45 UTC

OP

IT'S THE FUCKING TOP. SELL. SELL IT ALL.

Early replies (first 3)

62422571
cringest boomer in the world

ameripoors have the worst presidents seriously

old senile pedo
black muslim
orange cheetoh

62422572
Fuck you tom I refuse to kiss your ass for high wages. !

62422573
It's actually over...

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62422575
Doesn't the president always expect stocks to rise? Or is tweeting this an admission that in the absence of a such tweet he expects stocks to go down?

62422581
>>62422564
Historically what has happened when he made these types of posts?

62422597
Just checked his truth social btw and he didn't actually post this shit

62423042
>>62422564
ameridemons deserve their suffering

62423046
>>62423042
true

62423047
every time trump says buy its goes up btw

62423063
Its a commentary account you literal brownoids

62423067
>>62423047
Im waiting for the day you do that to me. You have no idea. Stonk.

62423076
there's no reason for this shit to go up from here.

62423086
>>62422564
Zion Don is always right when it comes to stocks though despicable as he is . Cheers to a green day!

62423099
>>62422564
>DonaldTrumpNat
>Commentary account
I don't frequent X, is that his actual account or some clown with a purchased checkmark?

62423113
>>62423099
It's some clown making up tweets for engagement, trump didn't post that shit on his truth social, people are just blatantly lying on x now because it gives them more engagement and in turn more money

62423120
>>62423047
Everything that trump touches turns into shit btw.

62423147
>>62422564
i did last week
-40% ytd

62423155
>>62423113
it's even worse, OP reposted this from reddit.

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:29 UTC

Early replies (first 3)

62420679
Can you tokenize land and earn yield with a burger tho?

62420684
>>62420669
>>62420679
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62420685
its grim
need to dump then pump to new moon!
>im out for now, getting back in under a $

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62420691
>>62420679

62420719
>>62420691
McDonald’s will make a mcfortune from tokenization, looks like

62420721
>>62420669

62420724
>>62420721
Hot

62420765
>>62420679
yeah, they're building a wendy's near me

62420880
to the surprise of no one

62421586
>>62420669
Verbose xrp shill summoning ritual

62421780
>>62420669
Bullish

62421783
>>62420719

they are called shares and we have been trading them on markets since 1602 but as far back as like 2000 BC people invested in trade and received compensations for their investments

>muh token

yeah whatever faggot

62421790
>>62421783
I have my own land to tokenize you dumb faggot

62421870
>>62420721
not into feet but that does look quite tasty

62421882
>>62420721
I like feet but I hate this

62421933
BQQQQQQQQQQQQQQQQQQQM

62422051
>>62421933
came here to say this

62422089
>>62420721
I hate footfags so much

62422734
>>62420669
Best thread on biz kek
t. Xrp baggie

62422736
>>62422734
so bullish xrp

62423118
>>62420721
ok this looks appealing, but I need the sweaty feet scent and I feel like the burger scent is going to sour the experience

item.title: Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco… — 7 replies — 28.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss Bitco…

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:40 UTC

OP

Thinking seriously of finally capitulating and selling my 12,000 DOT at ATL of $0.80 and realizing a 100k net loss

Bitcoin is more likely to go to 500k than dot going to $8

It's over

What do you think

Early replies (first 3)

62423104
Why on earth did you invest in that garbage in the first place?

62423107
>>62423104
My friend convinced me. Wish he was never born. Cocksucker

62423116
>>62423107
it must be traumatizing, I have seen you in like 4 different threads. And I actually do believe you, no LARP goes for this long. There will be more opportunities, maybe it's not in equities or speculative "assets" this time.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423121
>>62423107
honestly dude this sounds insane but in the sheer absolute bumfuck rarity that DOT recovers. you're going to commit suicide if it moons. I know this sounds highly irrational but $9600 is inconsequential money unless you have debt. it sounds fucking nuts but maybe just park it safely and just genuinely forget about it.

62423122
>>62423121
>you're going to commit suicide if it moons
if you realize losses now*

this is purely psychological, it's shit financial advice and it's a gamblers fallacy but im telling you the way you're talking about this shows me if DOT moons you will not cope. It's already traumatizing missing out on the semiconductor/AI boom then having to miss the very asset you invested in recover would make ME want to blow my brains out and I have never had suicidal depression

62423140
>>62423116
>>62423121
>>62423122
Least insane DOT baggie

62423143
>>62423140
dumb misunderstanding but I get it, I am just saying 88% loss is devastating. if it was options, people just let it expire at that point

item.title: ALTERNATIVE CASH STREAMS / SIDE JOBS / AI GRIFT / SIDE HUSTLES — 20 replies — 25.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
ALTERNATIVE CASH STREAMS / SIDE JOBS / AI GRIFT / SIDE HUSTLES

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:05 UTC

OP

We all wanna make money on /biz/ right? Instead of chasing returns or making big bets can we discuss actual proven ways to make some money on the side? I have been trying to find easy, not effortless mind you just easy, ways to make money on the side. The total doesn’t have to be big, just consistent. Does anyone have ideas? If it could be something friendly to a noob in that particular field, even better
I know stuff like drop shipping is a fucking scam, flipping Facebook marketplace stuff is not for me
The most common examples you see online are donating plasma and using online survey sites
Does anyone know of anything better, maybe with AI? If so please explain it in detail with examples of how much money you’ve made.
Oh and one more thing YES I HAVE A SALARY JOB AND ALREADY GOT A RAISE FOCUS ON THIS BEING ABOUT SIDE MONEY

Early replies (first 3)

62416647
There still a market for banana-in-your-butt on chaturbate or onlyfans.

Or there's always wendy's

62416661
>>62416647
Ummm how would one make money with Wendy’s silly

62417058
oil is rugging

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62417092
>>62416542
foot modelling

62417962
.

62418176
It's hit and miss and slow, but over the last year I've won $100 playing slot machine apps. I haven't deposited any money in the apps either.

62419720
Bumping

62419807
Mow your neighbors’ lawns you lazy bastard

62420024
>>62419807
theres already companies doing that for citycucks

62420042
>>62416542
what sites even pay for surveys?

62420049
>>62420042
prolific

62420106
>>62419720
https://www.dataannotation.tech/
never tried it, i just found it

62421306
ANYONE

62421335
>>62418176
Yeah? How's that work? They keep a tally of what you owe and you just never pay it if your winnings exceed what you owe? And what apps do you like to use?

62421341
>>62420106
They are fairly selective, but hire people in waves every couple months iirc. I've worked a year on the site and the work has gotten more difficult as AI advances. Requires some focus and brainpower for most tasks nowadays. Less a side hustle and more a part time job imo. If you have specialty knowledge from a degree like stem or law tho could be a great gig until AI is advanced enough to not need you.

62421350
>>62420049
Hate that site. Everything is automated including whatever the hell they use to decide to ban people. Feels like the whole thing is just ran by chatgpt hallucinating everything.

62421354
>>62420106
these things are absolute garbage unless you live in india

62422162
>>62416542
Just get a part time job. I make $18 dollars an hour working part time a pizza hut as a cook 9 hours a week, and I can get all the free pizza I want.

62422265
I remember on /b/ like 15 years ago we would have these google adsense threads where we would post our fake blogs with google ads enabled and click on each others ads and get money out of it. It was decent spending money at the time. I didn't have the diligence to do it everyday so I stopped.

62423053
>>62422265
So what you’re saying is we need to put our autistic minds together and start scamming Google? Got it

62423085

Damn this bitch knows game & love dick. Im hooked. Payment? Lol

Monero Monday

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:49 UTC

OP

We're back, bitches. Monero Monday. If we all buy XMR weekly, we'll all pump each other's bags while also riding the wave of any future hacks (they're inevitable)

Save your dying value from the memes and shit, swap to XMR

Early replies (first 3)

62423149
>>62423137
buy an ad.

62423165
>>62423149
I did. This thread is an ad. I paid in sweat & time. Eternal currencies.


Now help me, and yourself, make money (stack XMR) or fuck off

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:11 UTC

OP

Doge Army manifesting $1 DOGE OOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO

Early replies (first 3)

62418650
OWIWOWOWOWOWOWOWOW

62418728
shib to 1 cent

62421659
>>62418531
4. Cents or dollars. Call it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62423012
Them bags getting heavy?

62423071

item.title: VOO and Chill — 130 replies — 355.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
VOO and Chill

Open thread • Replies: 130 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:31 UTC

OP

The more I look into investing and compare the historical returns of certain ETFs / individual stocks it seems like the whole philosophy of “VOO and Chill” is equal parts really smart and retarded
It’s like a no brainer for money but even now as long as the market is in a growth period shouldn’t you at least do QQQ?
It seems so simple that’d you miss a lot of upside but the returns are honestly not bad and extremely consistent
How accurate is this assessment? It’s both so simple and guaranteed that everyone should it but also too safe so you end up missing out on more upside
I’m talking 5-20 years investing here not trading

Early replies (first 3)

62400515
Boomer funds are gay and lame

62400554
>>62400515
What qualifies as one
Why
What crypto do you want to shill

62400560
boglehead method is the tried and tested way, buy the market with an index fund that has a low fee, invest monthly, do it consistently for decades and you WILL be rich
but biz/anons don't care about being rich when they're older, they want to be rich NOW
greed is a deadly sin

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62419932
>>62419709
>>62419916
That said, I also pile up a bunch of ETFs for additional diversification. People hate on diversification these days, and even Warren Buffett says derisively that diversification is protection from ignorance. I’m happy with that. Win in my book

62419947
What about things like DRAM and SOXX
Especially SOXX since it invests in the entirety of the AI infrastructure, wouldn't that be a big bet vs just going into the market

62419965
>>62419947
Semiconductors as a sector is basically guaranteed up and to the right over the long term but look at the chart right now. It would be completely unsurprising IF people who buy here after this insane pump are stuck in a negative position for many years, even a decade. Happens a lot

62419969
>>62419965
Yeah...I keep missing the boat lol.....and now it seems like Apple is asking to buy chinese memory chips so I wonder if Im lucky to not be in micron rn

62419980
By the way guys, here's a cool free tool to backtest ETF compositions:
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com/backtest-portfolio#analysisResults

I forgot the exact ratio but I read that tech plus small caps combo is gold tier returns wise and it seemed to be true when I last played with this tool.

>>62419969
If you get frustrated it's best to just buy the whole market imho. You get better returns than most people (average retail investor only gets around 5% CAGR) and get to keep your time and peace of mind

62419989
>>62419980
im fine with doing this shit long term but do have slight fomo about all this stuff being a once in a lifetime money making opportunity....but i guess if that were true the residual "go up and right" should also last a while

62420075
>negative correlation between amount of text in a post and intelligence of that post

62420076
>>62420075
Youre gay lol

62420079
>>62420076
no u

62420460
>>62420079
owned

62420887
>>62420075
Brevity is the soul of wit.

62421697
Anyone here going into DRAM

62421773
VOO and chill is the best because it's the most safe/leaning aggressive fund you can get. Alternatives:
>individual stocks: this always looks great in hindsight, but who the fuck knew that Micron was going to skyrocket? And if you picked Microsoft you would be underperforming VOO
>volatile ETFs like BAKA, DRAM, TQQQ: these are great when the market is up, but when you lose 15% in a few days you will feel it
>BTC, ETH: yeah right, the S&P beats these easily (inb4 "zoom out", nobody cares, that kind of increase will never be duplicated)

62421787
>>62420887
Brevity: Wit's soul

62421799
>"just VOO and chill bro"
>gets caught in a 5+ yr -60% drawdown event (2008) with no hedges to cover his balls
boyhood is regurgitating the VOO mantra
adulthood is meticulously engineering your poorfolio to get within 80% of index performance while cutting the drawdown periods by half

62421813
>>62400493
Or I can throw a good amount into Uranium miner indexes and chill to retire in half the time

62422318
>>62419913
>I even did completely mong shit like having a python script take a screenshot of every known stock loaded into eval sites like GuruFocus etc and then simply counting the green pixel ratio.
kek

62422375
>>62421799
ok Mr Buffett, what's an alternative long term investment strategy then?

62422392
>>62400493
Stock picking is still good and valid retard.
>But I'm losing % if I don't buy voo
This is smol brain thinking.

62422418
>>62422392
Im down about 100$ since starting my high risk portfolio a few weeks ago.
The low risk portfolios doing p good tho

62422766
>>62421799
Alright tell me the next big thing then
I see a lot of talk about the memory bottle neck happening through 2027/2028 is DRAM a safe bet

62422806
>>62422766
i use a modified barbell strategy that i call "dick and balls" method.

>40% high risk high return ETFs
this is your right nut and drives your explosive folio growth. you want trending sector or thematic ETFs here.

>40% value ETFs with low low beta
this is your left nut and provides slow, steady growth even during volatile conditions. stuff like SCHD would fall in here.

>20% ultrasafe assets like bonds and treasurys
this is your shaft, without which your balls have no raison d'etre. this will grow steadily as you grow older and provide the foundation upon which your entire portfolio stands erect.

when you're young, you take ballsy risks, hence the 40-40 balls.
as you get older, you seek wisdom and steady upwards thrust, hence your 20% shaft grows year by year.

use a portfolio backtester/optimizer to determine the exact composition of each component. you can get FAR less max drawdown than plain S&P while retaining most of returns. the right nut can be configured to catch seasonal upsides, i.e. whatever's hottest at the moment.

62422917
>>62422806
Okay but as someone with 55K sitting in VOO, just DCA each week, what should I look towards next
As Stated I was considering just beginning to go into DRAM for now and selling within 1 to 2 years

62422933
It's retarded unless you have like 2 million.
If you don't you have to take gambles.
Time is more important than money.

62422968
>>62422933
right but if you start young then you have time on your side so all you need to do is focus on making more money
it's an equation that always balances out, risk takes principal out of the equation and time

62422991
>>62421799
this is legitimately not true when compared to other indexes

62423039
>>62421799
>he thinks there will be another 08 event

62423048
>>62423039
have you seen the SP chart? Have you seen the state debt? have you seen the bond yield inversion?

62423061
>>62423039
>>62423048
Why does it matter if there is another 08 unless you plan to retire in 5-10 years

62423124
>>62422991
haha yeah

>>62423061
because your primary source of income would dry up under such conditions and you'll have to liquidate and partially realize your losses to pay your immediate obligations you chuckelfucking retard
>but i have cash reserves
really? enough to last you multiple years? i don't think so.

item.title: UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1 — 170 replies — 235.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
UNDER $10K NET WORTH THREAD #1

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-06-28 17:09 UTC

OP

Brothers,

SpaceX has begun to rise. Its success or fall is of little importance to us in the short term.

In the meanwhile we must devise ways to gain a spot at the table with our kin in the six figure club.

I for one propose we spend our time learning a subject. Knowledge is the greatest wealth of all.

However, I would be remiss to not tell the truth that I haven't a clue what we should study.

So instead I suggest we create an eggregore for these threads. Others made monero-chan, bobo and sminem

I am thinking we create one based on a girl who would be willing to believe in us while we are poor and brave the pain throughout having to witness our more disappointing moments of action. Never losing faith in us, she is always there. Yes. This is how we shall spend our time.

At any rate, that is my suggestion for tonight's fun. I have to work in the morning so let's not get too rowdy. Take it or leave it. If you will take it, then be sure to double it and pass it on to the next anon.

Let us commence the general!

Come one, come all!

Early replies (first 3)

62373772
>>62373752
Jannies will surely delete these threads. Instead of creating an eggregore why not try something new and usurp an old one. It may be a derivative of one which has already been made.

62373798
>>62373772
Hello anon thanks for joining. You are poster #1. Your anime image shall be the eggregore. The "coffee" in the image is rather a good omen. We all know images with hot girls accompanied by a brief post on coffee attracts all the attention.

Now that matter is settled, help me add some links to this post here. This will be the header for the start of each general

Helpful links

>Educational materials
https://w9.kaijimanga.com/

>Looking to try the TCG route?
https://youtube.com/@alphainvestments69?si=tDAPjnRSZhyCIGP5

>Lost all hope?
https://www.indeed.com/


>Hidden in plain sight
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Limitless_(film)

>Games to strengthen you financial acumen
https://mahjongsoul.yo-star.com/

>Learn your from your betters
https://ia600609.us.archive.org/3/items/the-house-of-rothschild-ferguson-niall/The%20House%20of%20Rothschild%20-%20Ferguson%2C%20Niall.pdf
What is the name of that character in your post?

62373799
>>62373772
verily, a true fact

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62403337
>>62401765
This general also is a work in progress, I am hoping 10 generals from now we have a much better work flow, plan and ultimately success. In some capacity.

62404260
Bump

62404574
No point in this general. Fastest way to make 10k is just to work for a bit, you shouldn’t be investing

62404709
Bump

62404737
>>62373752
its a spacex thread?
its time to buy o better buy in few days one week

62405715
bump

62407354
Page 8 bump, /biz/ must be losing money again because there's an uptick in activity.

62407450
almost 9 months since i started my first job after college
this feels stagnant and joyless
what exactly do i need to do to escape wageslavery and poverty

62407931
Someone send me ten grand so I can join the club, thanks

62408558
>>62407450
What were you doing in 2017, 2021 and December of 2024?

62408565
>>62407354
Thank you, my wage job prevents me from dedicating my energies to this thread but I should have some time soon

62408611
>>62408584
Wtf are you on about dude, yes I made 54 posts over the span of 8 days. I'm surprised you're not taking your own advice and leaving 4chan.

62410044
Bump

62410051
Under 10k you just need to have a job and save cash.
You need cash flow and if you dont have shit a job is the only way to start.

62410731
Bump

62411520
>>62408558
>2017
still in high school
>2021
still in uni
>December of 2024
just graduated and was looking for jobs (i still had 0 dollars to my name at that point)

62411614
>>62373752
>>62373772
>>62373798
>>62373799
>>62373803
>post Maccari's "Cicero Denounces Catiline" (1888)
>crop out Catiline
Fuck you OP, I'm glad you're poor.

62412314
>>62411520
You could've played the memecoin casino in 2021.

62413842
Bump, I'll be free tomorrow

62416357
Bump

62417812
page 9 bump. Don't say I never did anything for you.

62418065
Almost the weekend then I can devote my energies to this thread.

And a big thank you to the poster above this post, looks like you were based after all

62418133
Bump

62418220
I got out and made tens of dollars this week with MU and SNDK. Lost some with WDC.

62419326
This is very stupid but an anon in some other thread weeks ago shilled me TNYA. He gave no explanation, no catalyst, no description what the company even does but he said that we’ll be eating good in like 1-2 years I don’t even remember exactly when. But hey it’s a cheap stock and he sounded confident to me

62419390
go all in hbar when it bottoms at 0.03

62419964
Bump, just woke up, let's get started

62420874
It would appear that everyone is talking about the new WENDYs Stock

Naturally we should take some time to consider this potential moonshot. Could be a waste of time. In a few I will post information I find relevant to the stock and whether investing is the right option

62422903
Another page 9 bump

62423064
Good to see the thread is still going strong. Today is my off day, let's look into the next subject "predictive programming"

We'll start with Oliver tree

item.title: Why aren't you holding BTC? — 101 replies — 36.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren't you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 101 • Updated: 2026-06-28 15:09 UTC

OP

Too expensive? Prefer alts? Don't trust it? Or do you simply think there are better opportunities elsewhere?
Curious to hear your thoughts.

Early replies (first 3)

62395695
you can get better gains on stocks. those companies work for your money. btc sits there doing nothing but has the same risk profile as sharing a hobos needle without greater fools left to carry it to $200k

62395915
give me reasons to hold btc.

62395925
I am holding because I bought in 2020 before etfs now I refuse to sell because I'll get audit raped by the CRA supposedly so I'm stuck holding until it's got better tax laws or borrowing rates.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62415296
>>62395655
Bitcorn has no liquidity now - there are no baggies left to trap.

I'd rather gamble on stocks - at least the price actually moves.

62415297
>>62396879
There is 'demand' for literally meme garbage like PEPE and DOGE which do nothing - not really an argument.

62415301
>>62415217
No, but MSTR collapse will cause a negative doom loop for Bitcorn if they have to start dumping.

62415492
>>62415228
Are you white?

62416132
>>62395655
I sold between 90-110k
Bought back between 59-64k
Still 1/3 of Tether left to buy further dips

62416334
>>62411213
Yeah. The Aegis partnership makes me even more interested to see how the TBV public testnet progresses.

62416338
>>62415301
but short term

62416355
>>62416132
Nice trades.

62416385
>>62395655
because I make money trading not holding garbage

62416439
>>62416385
Because I believe Bitcoin is the strongest long-term store of value in crypto. Short-term volatility doesn't change why I bought it in the first place.

62416488
>>62411265
I'm not holding bitcoin for next week. I'm holding it for the next decade.

62416497
>>62411213
I have doubts for Aave coin

62416506
Because every major dip has looked scary in the moment, but the long-term trend is what matters to me.

62416528
eistein coin

62417470
>>62416385
Garbage? Seriously?

62417484
>>62416497
Nobody's saying you have to like the coin. The point is the protocol and why it still dominates DeFi lending.

62417496
>>62411241
your choice

62417853
>>62415297
Memecoins are different

62419146
>>62395655
Because I'm not a retard

62419150
I know the maxis wont want to watch this…but btc is fundamentally flawed, as are all pow and pos consensus mechanisms…it’s not gonna work long term
https://m.youtube.com/watch?v=fo8ZScrXFZE&ra=m
Jesus loves you, Christ is King

62419226
>>62395655
I dont hold anything with zero value...maybe trade it

62419255
>>62419226
That's funny. From $1 to $100k and zero value?

62419275
>>62419255
Zero value as in speculative/doesnt generate a cashflow...sorry you know nothing about finance.

62420185
>>62419146
Non coiner. Ok

62421786
>>62419150
Hmm, that's your opinion.

62422222
>>62409106
>he doesnt realize 1 mcmansion is not enough and they need the price to go up into infinity
fucking bitcuck

62422228
Holy shit guys. sound the alarm bell. Someone just pointed out that managers in a company want to get paid for their efforts... No wonder why all stocks in the world are in a perpetual downtrend. We've finally figured it out, all thanks to a bitcuck. It was the damn managers ALL ALONG! using the stocks as a money printer. Better fucking buy some BITCOIN to get myself out of this mess. Lord what have I done? This market failure is finally solved by BITCOIN. Oh lord thank you lord. I have seen the light. Thank god for saving me from those evil damn managers who keep dumping their stocks on me. Trillions of liquidity, all harvested by CEOs... LET BANKS FALL! BITCOIN TO DA MOON!

62422277
>>62422228
So you just realized just NOW?

62422765
>>62419275
People only need x1000 so it doesn't matter.

62422776
>>62416506
??

item.title: >only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX — 43 replies — 26.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable >now we know the price floor is $150 now is the time to buy SPCX

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-28 16:15 UTC

OP

>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
>now we know the price floor is $150
now is the time to buy SPCX

Early replies (first 3)

62408493
>>62408480
Bagholder cope.

62408507
>>62408493
No cope saar only gains to be made! Please buy more stocks from this very profitable company! Time is limited for maximum gains must hurry before money train takes off!

62408519
>>62408480
>>only down 4% from its ipo,
kek about to 50% in 6 months

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62414040
>>62408480
should I buy in now? I want to hold some before starship 13

62414121
>>62408480
with only 5% of the float available or w/e. holy shit yall are fucked when it opens up

62414137
>>62408480
Melon got re-bogged and fattened which he only does when things are going badly for him
2x short because of this

62414157
>>62408480
>We privatized NASA and now we want you to pay us to see Hollywood for scientists productions
Yeah, nah. I saw Kubrick admit to faking the moon landing.

62414178
>>62408480
There's genuinely not enough data to make a principled model of it. However the only thing not hypey about SpaceX is StarLink, which is a genuine business. Merging xAI into SpaceX however just tied a 1 ton ball and chain around it, because it's an insatiable blackhole of capex with no clear upside.

62415681
>>62414137
he was spending too much time being a master of POE2 instead of going to the gym. he'll get back on the horse.

62415707
This thread didn't age well.

62415715
>>62415707
$150 is still the floor. it'll be $300 by hanukkah

62415763
>>62411812
Holy baggie, this garbage isn't worth a fraction of what it is, why would your delusional number be even close to the floor

62416005
>>62408480
90x earnings is INSANE. Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity. Equivalent to buying Worldcomm with the exception that Space X probably has a longterm future, but is at absolute bubble top with an ABSURD valuation and no course to revenues in the near future, kept aloft by virtue of its multiple rules changed inclusion in indices receiving passive flows and retail hype that has come and gone.

62416247
>>62416005
>>62415763
it's already up for the day!

62416343
>>62416005
>Investing in this is a sure marker you are retarded and willful exit liquidity

while true, never underestimate the retardation of the retail masses

62416471
>>62408480
Keep buy Bagholders, keep buying!

62416737
>>62408480
I haven't seen a single bull "case" that has used numbers for buying Spcx. Meanwhile all Bears have to do is look at that those Quarterly and Y/Y financials directly below OP's image they were conveniently edited out.

62416873
>>62415707
Yep people of /biz/ seem to be unable to value this asset correctly. The SpaceMarines are back!!

62417077
doubters in shambles

62417086
AI FUCKING DATA CENTERS IN SPACE!

62417121
>>62408480
IT JUST KEEPS TUMBLIMG DOWN TUMBLIMG DOWN
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oIscL-Bjsq4

BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
BAGGIES ARE YOU OK
ARE YOU OK BAGGIES


Did you guys manage to sell in thegreen be honest?

62417135
>>62408480
ITS OVER

62417520
>>62417077
Yep, truly in shambles. Another down day.

Even if SpaceX is an amazing compnay, entry point matters. SpaceX market cap at $1 trillion is when it starts to get somewhat attractive. Just be patient young Padawon.

62418371
>>62408480
>only down 4% from its ipo, which is very reasonable
okay so you expected 30% by now... or what..... what is this point? this isnt even cope it's just weird

62419735
>>62408480
The price floor was always $150.
If it's below that at (can't remember date) they can't sell more stock.
So it's got to be $150 by that date. Someone can find it. Don't care.

62419742
isn't most of retail locked in until precisely the point where early investors are legally allowed to sell and cash in at any price? oh yes they are.

62419746
>>62414178
the listing has a TAM of equivalent of the total value of the us economy, or the the money spent on food by the planet, every year, year on year, to pay for its ai services. are elons data centres worth more than the entire us economy, or the entire global food chain supply, now or in the future?

62419817
>>62419746
Qualifier.
Do I live in a machine in the future?

62419957
OP bought the top and is now panicking to sell the bottom

62420705
>>62419957
This is the vibe I'm getting. I really hope you guys didn't see the most obvious pump and dump scheme in history and still fall for it like an indian who can see the train coming yet still gets hit by it.

62421234
Imagine when starship fails, imagine when you can dump without IPO penalty.

62421347
>>62419957
not even remotely close to the bottom

62422910
>>62417520
More like $200bn MC,

item.title: polkadogshit — 37 replies — 23.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
polkadogshit

Open thread • Replies: 37 • Updated: 2026-06-28 16:18 UTC

OP

$0.80 ATL

Early replies (first 3)

62422018
if you invested $1000 in DOT 5 years ago. you'd have $20 now.

62422036
I lost over 100k on this shit

62422050
>>62422036
pain

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62422091
>>62422086
>Despair creates the opposite of energy
nah you have to channel it, I know it sounds faggy and bloomerpilled but it can really be a source of energy. it's called spite.
>Nevertheless I am in a decent place thanks to the grace of God allowing me to earn a good living
good, in this job market? good man I mean it.
>Not a day goes by though where I don't get consumed by all I've lost
are you dead? then it's not over son.

62422094
>>62422091
>it's called spite.

Probably this has been the driving force of my life for many years now. It's just my normal at this point

62422096
>>62422094
I guess you had the energy all along, you just didn't know it. Been the same for me, silver linings everywhere. I guess it's the opposite of getting rich and "winning the game" then getting ironically bored. Instead we have insane regret of lost potential of generational wealth, which drives us to a permanent state of unable being bored with the cost of yelling into our pillows and driving 140 mph on the interstate

62422101
>>62422096
I am consumed by regret. The other day I logged into my coin base and saw my losses and nearly physically wept

It's an assload of money that can never be recovered and represents the best years of my life which I have slaved away for making that money only to lose it all to crypto rug pull grifters and I will never have my revenge. It's all very discouraging and in the end forces one to accept what a fucking retard he is or at least has been

62422114
>>62422101
I feel you, it's real.

It's more common than you think, people don't say or admit it. I promise you there were people who sold MU or sandisk super early thinking "ok this is the top, let me take my gains" and then watched as it continued to sky rocket far beyond even GME.

that's why they say traders need an iron will, I certainly dont fucking have it actually. I don't think I will and I don't have any interest. If I ever have a decent amount of net liq, i'd probably yolo 2-5% of it every couple of months into some silly options trade then get my fix and leave. I actually hate gambling as it turns out.

just become the house, take the landlordpill or the datacenterpill. this sheer regret is making me reconsider my moral compass sure but you and I need to fucking recover from this. So yeah maybe that means creating a business that perhaps caters to some tasty government contracts given that you look the other way of what it does. Because fucking hell this pain lasts for years, decades I hear unless you fucking get to a point of avenging the pain. god damn it all bro, justified greed at this point.

62422118
>>62422114
No, the only solution is to sell your bags and never look back. The more you chase winning back what you lost, the more you will lose

62422119
>>62422118
>The more you chase winning back what you lost, the more you will lose
brother that's what I mean by "becoming the house". fuck the gambling. the only investing should be done in index and just never looking at it and forgetting its there. invest in stable income.

62422122
>>62422118
accepting defeat but then potentiating it by living in eternal poverty hell is not a choice. I can't do that man. I want a wife and kids and a paid off house, I can't not have it; I will work smart for it. I will grind only in high-yield. grinding low-yield is only better than truly giving up and NEETing

62422127
>>62422119
By the grace of God (I keep saying that even though I am unsure about a lot of things, it seems cautionary to do so) I have a big payday coming in after years of slaving and it's going straight into paying off my home loan. My accountant tried to talk me out of it and convince me to keep my "great loan" and invest it in long term accounts at the bank or something else. But I learned my lesson and never again fall for shiny promises. Keep it simple and expect the worst

62422132
>>62422122
If you want a paid off home one day, the last thing you should do it keep taking risks with your hard earned money

62422133
>>62422127
good, im not gonna pretend to be a financial expert. unironically ask opus 4.8 and give it all the details of your finances or consult some high-end (((financial advisor)))

62422136
>>62422132
I meant a business. I agree mate. but the average house cost where I live is $700k. maybe I should swallow my pride and move elsewhere but alas this is a good state. well at least I am only child so I will inherit the mortgage and another property; I never asked purely because it makes me uncomfortable to think of how much debt that business property we have has and the value of it, and our house itself. I have no idea and it's on purpose because I don't want to be stressed the fuck out right now until I have a job. im so fucking late at 24 man, and in this job market too I still have 2 years left to get a degree in EE. well silver lining im kinda glad to not graduate in this shit job market but its only a temporary relief that has no guarantee, it could easily get worse next year.

if the job market was bad during the "AI boom", why would it get better at the bust...?

62422138
>>62422132
business can definitely be risk, let me make that clear too lol

62422139
>>62422136
The most concerning part is that AI and shit is going to take our jobs

62422143
>>62422139
>The most concerning part is that AI and shit is going to take our jobs
it's enabling entrepreneurship far more. im spilling the beans (not really), but yes gov contracts are far more accessible than people realize. you just need to be a non-felon and clear basic clearance. you have to find the right shit for what you can provide though; and it can take a long ass time but its how many small businesses can start. even with low capital (again it depends and im being very very fucking generic about it)

62422146
>>62422143
I don't share your optimism but good luck.

62422149
>>62422146
thanks, optimism is high-yield. even in delusion. albeit it is more applicable socially than financially, i'll give you that. best of luck man.

62422153
>>62422149
Optimism leads to risk taking and losing bigly though. I think many people cannot conceptualize accurately what losing your life savings actually means and the fact that it can never be undone.

62422465
>>62422015
This piece of shit charged multiple dot just to move some coins back when it was $4 each. Can't imagine using it when it was $50 each.
Fucking useless vaporware.

62422552
>>62422015
Expect a bounce back after bear market

62422561
Protip for you baggies. Sell and never look back. Never check the price again. Stay ignorant. If it pumps or dumps from your sell date, it's irrelevant information to you that will only make you make worse decisions in the future.

However, when it comes to DOT. I must once again thank that faggot who started shilling it here, calling it /biz/s coin and the next LINK. I dumped my bags then and saved the absolute humiliation of this current price action. by which I have broken my own rule by following. But only because again, I must thank the deadshit poorfag shill for the encouragement to dump my bags.

62422646
>>62422561
Step aside Linkies. DOT is the official crypto of /biz/

62422659
>>62422015
Kek baggies.

There were threads in here a few weeks back saying "omg buy now it's under a dollar" - these people are buying literal air and being exit liq for VCs.

62422688
>>62422015
literally one of the worst investments imaginable

62422708
>>62422659
You better buy!

62422710
>>62422552
No its done

62422718
A shitton of /biz/tards bought this at $50 and they're still holding it, creating stupid threads everyday and seething at coins like hyperliquid

62422729
>>62422718
Guilty, and then DCA all the way down to 11 dollars and realized it's fucked

62422909
>>62422710
Heard the same cries in previous bear markets.....

62422921
>>62422659
Earlier in the year there were half a dozen shill threads posted around the same time. The price was $2 at the time. A reminder that when a shitcoin gets heavily shilled here, the top is already in.

item.title: AI Related ETFs and Fields - Not Missing The Boat — 13 replies — 7.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
AI Related ETFs and Fields - Not Missing The Boat

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-28 16:09 UTC

OP

Is Robotics the next big thing?
Memory and such have already gone up bigly
But I keep seeing news that robotics are going to be the next thing to keep an eye on
What’s /biz/‘s opinion on this, if you agree with the sentiment than what stocks or ETFs should someone look into?
Additionally on the topic of semiconductor ETFs like SOXX or memory ones like DRAM, would you recommend someone goes into those rather than a standard market ETF
I am talking not for short term gains but holding for at least a year or two, something comfortable - are they still a wise decision
I ask cause DRAM is new and who knows how long till keep going up, SOXX has a 25 yeah volatile history and idk what it’ll do in the next years
So basically, is robotics the next big thing and if or if not would you recommend those ETFs and for how long

Early replies (first 3)

62421506
>>62421297
nuclear energy smr is the new meta stocks LEU
SMR
OKLO
RYCEY
CEG
CCJ
BWXT

or etf SMRF

62421522
>>62421297
quantum ai space robot data center ram

62421538
>>62421506
why would this be true

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421564
>>62421538

''Advanced Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) are a key part of the Department’s goal to develop safe, clean, and affordable nuclear power options. The advanced SMRs currently under development in the United States represent a variety of sizes, technology options, capabilities, and deployment scenarios. These advanced reactors, envisioned to vary in size from tens of megawatts up to hundreds of megawatts, can be used for power generation, process heat, desalination, or other industrial uses. SMR designs may employ light water as a coolant or other non-light water coolants such as a gas, liquid metal, or molten salt''
https://www.energy.gov/ne/advanced-small-modular-reactors-smrs

>Japan is making investments in shipbuilding and fusion energy.

>China is focusing on improving energy resource security and has announced a five-year investment plan of $3 trillion to $3.8 trillion.

>India is looking to diversify its sources of energy imports and use technologies such as coal gasification and biofuels to reduce import dependence.
https://www.morganstanley.com/insights/articles/asia-energy-capex-9t-opportunity-2030

>Hyperscale AI data centers require city-scale electricity loads, making dependable baseload power a strategic necessity.

>Microsoft and Amazon are forming direct nuclear partnerships and pursuing advanced reactor technologies to secure long-term energy supply.

>Energy infrastructure, particularly nuclear generation and uranium supply, is emerging as a structural beneficiary of AI-driven demand growth
https://www.zerohedge.com/ai/ai-boom-powering-nuclear-renaissance

it doesn't matter whether the demand comes from robotics, data centers or space exploration the demand for energy especially clean energy increases every year

62421571
>>62421297

tl;dr: no, because there are no public robotics companies worth buying, and even if you could buy private robotics companies, the ones worth buying are chinese, so no way to buy that anyway.

people have been asking this for a while

at the end of the day, robots require really cheap robotics labor to manufacture/R&D, they don't need battery tech improvements, they don't need AI improvements, they don't need regulation decrease, all they need is just really cheap robotics labor. It requires a ton of iterations to get product market fit on a robot, and that is a function of robotics labor

By an order of magnitude, robotics labor is cheaper in china, and for all intents and purposes, this will stay this way.

so why the fuck would any robotics company want to seriously operate in the US when they can just operate in China?

There's no good answer for this, and since you and I can't buy private chinese companies, the answer to your question is robotics is only the next big thing in abstract, it can't exist in any meaningfully investable way

62421589
>>62421571
What is something se can invest in? I put all my eggs in the Link basket and... Well... Tbh the only reason that investment was successful at all was because I did 6 months of deep research on here with constant reading and asking in threads. Plus I didn't sell in 2021.

But basically I did well because of you guys as a hive mind where ideas and doubts were bashed around. I need your help again

62421677
>>62421564
Cool thanks for the sources

Anyway I thought about it and will just begin a position in DRAM I think

62421711
>>62421571
>because there are no public robotics companies worth buying,
Top KEK!
>>62421589
Ngmi especially if you listen to that retard AND stupid enough to have ever bought l*nk. You're much better off prompting Gemini.

62421737
>>62421297
A High up Microsoft executive told me their next big focus is at home helper robots.
Like a robot to do chores and shit.

I'm assuming AI plays a roll now

62422613
>>62421737
I’ve heard Chinese robots are the future

62422636
>>62421711
Well they got me to buy a make it stack of 100k so I'm still doing ok relatively speaking

62422664
BOOBA!!

62422897
>>62421297
Robotics is an engineering task and the timeframe is probably a bit long. A while ago Anthropic made a robo-hand which their AI could use/train on.. They abandoned the project because it kept breaking, and "coders" are not engineers..

With that being said, robotics are gonna scale with AI, and in time i think this will be where the money are..

item.title: >autistic cousin DCA'd social services payments into the market for 12 years while NEETing with his mom >threw his entir… — 10 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
>autistic cousin DCA'd social services payments into the market for 12 years while NEETing with his mom >threw his entir…

Open thread • Replies: 10 • Updated: 2026-06-28 16:30 UTC

OP

>autistic cousin DCA'd social services payments into the market for 12 years while NEETing with his mom
>threw his entire savings into BTC during the 2017 hype wave
>rode a 9x to 2025 before cashing out (while also DCAing into BTC from 2017 to 2025)
>now a millionaire thanks to taxpayers

How do some people have so much luck? He could have exited at any time but held until a few months before the crash. Meanwhile I bought high and sold low like a retard. Life is so unfair.

Early replies (first 3)

62421263
Fucking based.
Good for him.

62421310
>>62421254
>How do some people have so much luck?
A good player is always lucky.

62421376
>>62421254
>be my mom
>sold our house at the bottom of the market for $250k
>put $14,000 in her IRA 20 years ago with some scamfraud pseudo-brokerage, but forgot to choose her investments and now half of it is gone due to annual fees (found out last month)
>"why are you so upset? social security will keep paying me, i don't get what the issue is"
>social security's gonna get slashed in a few years
>now i'm her retirement plan and she's looking up filian responsibility laws

how come some people have none of the luck?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62421385
>>62421263
Dont be scared now. She is still kicking you out. Nigger.

62421390
>>62421254
I'd rather not have autism than be a millionaire. If anything, it's life being slightly more fair to him.

62421391
